mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where unemployment rises by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales indicates weakening aggregate demand that forces earnings downgrades and produces negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a win rate above 45% over any 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month industrial production growth during established expansion phases confirms capacity utilization strength and drives short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during expansion phases when industrial production rises.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with falling unemployment rates indicate a successful soft landing trajectory that supports equity valuations and generates positive monthly returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power when the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity that translates into positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth shows zero correlation with quarterly equity returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 250,000 in a single month confirm robust economic momentum that triggers immediate algorithmic buying and positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns turn negative in more than 50% of months where payroll growth surpasses the 250,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation readings above 2.5% force the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and generating negative monthly returns during high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% and the Federal Reserve maintains a dovish stance.
